title: Why AI Gets Your Instructions Wrong

modules.title: The Interpretation Gap: How LLMs Construct Meaning Differently Than Humans Do
modules.sub: Why AI reads your words but misses your meaning
modules.description: Learners examine the core mechanical difference between how large language models process text and how human readers interpret intent. They build a working mental model of token prediction and statistical pattern-matching that explains why fluent-sounding outputs can be fundamentally wrong.

modules.title: Ambiguity, Context Collapse, and the Failure Modes Professionals Hit Most
modules.sub: Mapping the recurring breakdowns in professional AI use
modules.description: Learners identify the most common failure patterns that emerge when workplace instructions lack explicit context or contain unresolved ambiguity. They analyze real examples of context collapse — where the AI fills gaps with plausible-but-wrong assumptions — and categorize which failure types their own work is most exposed to.

modules.title: Prompt Anatomy for Professionals: Constraints, Persona, Format, and Scope
modules.sub: Dissecting the structural components that drive better outputs
modules.description: Learners break down a professional-grade prompt into its functional parts — task, constraints, persona, output format, and scope — and practice assigning each element deliberately. They reconstruct underdefined prompts by diagnosing which components are missing and inserting them with precision.

modules.title: Diagnosing Bad Output: Is It the Model, the Prompt, or the Task Design?
modules.sub: A triage framework for tracing output failures to their source
modules.description: Learners apply a structured diagnostic process to a set of flawed AI outputs, distinguishing between failures caused by model limitations, poorly constructed prompts, and fundamentally ill-suited task design. They leave with a repeatable triage checklist they can apply immediately when outputs disappoint in real work.

modules.title: Domain-Specific Failure Patterns: Legal, Financial, Technical, and Managerial Contexts
modules.sub: How professional domains each expose distinct AI vulnerabilities
modules.description: Learners examine the characteristic ways AI misinterpretation manifests in legal, financial, technical, and managerial work, where precision and consequence are highest. They map domain-specific risk patterns to the structural prompt causes identified earlier in the course and adapt their diagnostic lens to their own professional field.

modules.title: Instruction Design as a Professional Skill: Iterative Refinement Frameworks
modules.sub: Treating prompt-writing as a disciplined, improvable craft
modules.description: Learners practice a systematic iteration process for refining instructions across multiple rounds, using structured evaluation criteria rather than intuition-based trial and error. They apply two refinement frameworks — constraint tightening and scope narrowing — to progressively improve outputs on a realistic professional task.

modules.title: Apply It: Audit and Rewrite Your Own High-Stakes AI Prompts
modules.sub: Bringing the full framework to bear on your real work
modules.description: Learners select actual prompts from their own professional practice, run them through the diagnostic and anatomical frameworks developed across the course, and produce rewritten versions with documented reasoning for each change. This capstone module produces a personally relevant artifact that demonstrates the course's full learning outcome.

modules.title: Organizational Implications: Setting Standards for AI Instruction Across Teams
modules.sub: Scaling individual skill into team-wide instruction quality
modules.description: Learners examine what happens when inconsistent prompt quality creates variable or risky outputs across an organization, and they draft the components of a lightweight team-level instruction standard. They consider governance questions — who owns prompt quality, how standards are documented, and how they evolve — and produce a starter framework applicable to their own team context.

synopsis: AI models fail professionals not because they are unintelligent, but because they interpret instructions through a fundamentally different process than human readers — one that is sensitive to phrasing, ordering, omission, and context in ways that are non-obvious and consequential. This course gives professionals a diagnostic framework for understanding why outputs go wrong and the instruction design skills to prevent it.
rationale: Misinterpreted AI instructions are one of the highest-frequency failure points in professional AI adoption, yet most guidance focuses on 'better prompts' without equipping professionals to understand the underlying interpretive mechanisms causing errors.
learning_outcome: Professionals can diagnose why an AI model misinterpreted a workplace instruction, identify the structural cause, and rewrite the instruction using a systematic framework that reduces misinterpretation risk in high-stakes professional contexts.
